chunks.page_summary: This page provides an overview of the Rheonics SRD-X7 RP Reactor Probe Density and Viscosity Meter, detailing its design, applications, available configurations, and key dimensions.
product_names: SRD-X7 RP Reactor Probe Density and Viscosity Meter
Rheonics Density and Viscosity meter SRD
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863
SRD-X7 RP Reactor Probe Density and Viscosity Meter
Modified on: Wed, 14 May, 2025 at 5:55 PM

What products are involved?
Rheonics Density and Viscosity meter SRD (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-density-meter-srd/)

What is the purpose of this article?
To give an overview of the Reactor Probe (-RP) version, -X7 configuration, of the SRD sensor and installation information.

1. Overview
The Rheonics Reactor Probe (RP) design for the SRD is a long insertion probe with a 19mm body diameter, configurable by total “L” length and flexible (adjustable) process connection, with PTFE-PEEK or Stainless Steel 316L compression fittings. Its order code is -X7.
The Rheonics SRD is an industrial sensor used to measure with high accuracy the density and viscosity of a fluid inline, to enable monitoring and control. Rheonics offers different variants for the SRD to adjust the sensor probe to the user’s needs and application requirements, see more here (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000297659).
The SRD-X7 variant is suited for installations in bioreactor tanks and glass reactors using a long insertion probe with compression fittings of 1” NPT, 3/4” NPT and others in two material options PTFE and Stainless Steel.
PTFE-PEEK fittings allow a variable insertion length (distance from fitting to sensor tip) since the fitting can be moved along the shaft of the probe. Stainless Steel compression fittings are generally fixed in a position once they are compressed.
The SRD Reactor Probe is defined by the total length, there are 4 options available: 150, 250, 350 and 450mm. The client can also define the compression fitting required that will be placed at some point along the shaft once received. The probe can be ordered without a compression fitting as well.

Figure 1: Reactor Probe Density and Viscosity Meter with 3/4" NPT PTFE Compression Fitting.

Figure 2: SRD-X7 Reactor Probe Dimensions mm [in.]

Specifications
key_facts: The SRD-X7 RP Reactor Probe is a long insertion probe with a 19mm body diameter.
The probe's total 'L' length is configurable, and it features a flexible (adjustable) process connection.
Available compression fitting materials for the probe include PTFE-PEEK or Stainless Steel 316L.
The order code for this specific Reactor Probe version is -X7.
The Rheonics SRD is an industrial sensor designed for high-accuracy inline measurement of density and viscosity.
The SRD-X7 variant is specifically suited for installations in bioreactor tanks and glass reactors.
Compression fittings for the SRD-X7 variant come in options like 1” NPT and 3/4” NPT, with material choices of PTFE and Stainless Steel.
PTFE-PEEK fittings enable a variable insertion length as they can be moved along the probe's shaft.
Stainless Steel compression fittings are typically fixed in position once compressed.
The SRD Reactor Probe is available in four standard total lengths: 150mm, 250mm, 350mm, and 450mm.
Clients have the option to specify the required compression fitting or order the probe without one.

chunks.page_summary: This page details specifications, available CAD files, installation possibilities, and principles for Rheonics SRD-X7 Reactor Probes, an inline viscometer and density sensor.

Drawings: SR-RP Configuration Drawing (https://rheonics.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Rheonics_SR-RP_Reactor-Probe.pdf)

SRD-RP CAD files
Sensor Variant | Drawing | CAD File

SRD-X7-RP350 | SRD-X7-RP350 Drawing (https://rheonics.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/Rheonics_SRV-RP350.pdf) (https://rheonics.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/11/Rheonics_SRD-X7-RP350.pdf) | SRD-X7-RP350 CAD File (https://rheonics.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/11/Rheonics_SRD-X7-RP350.zip) (https://staging.rheonics.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/Rheonics_SRV-RP350.zip)
SRD-X7-RP450 | SRD-X7-RP450 Drawing (https://rheonics.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/Rheonics_SRV-RP450.pdf) (https://rheonics.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/11/Rheonics_SRD-X7-RP450.pdf) | SRD-X7-RP450 CAD File (https://rheonics.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/11/Rheonics_SRD-X7-RP450.zip) (https://staging.rheonics.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/Rheonics_SRV-RP450.zip)

Some installation possibilities for the SRD Reactor Probe are:
* Installation in reactors, in particular bioreactors
* Installations with variable insertion length
* Installation in small pipe sizes (e.g. 1", 1.25" ID pipes)
* Installation in pipe sizes bigger than 1.5" with reduced (compared to -X5 sensors (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000411102-srv-x5-long-insertion-probe) ) cross section reduction inside the pipe due to probe.

Figure 3: SRD-X7 Reactor Probe comparison with SRD-X5.

2. Installation principles
The Rheonics SRD-X7 can be easily installed and integrated into an existing setup. Next considerations should be followed to ensure accurate, repeatable and reproducible density and viscosity readings.

Ensure sensing element in red is in contact with fluid
Avoid dead or stagnation zones in the sensing (red) area
SRD-RP tip needs a clearance of 12 mm [0.5"] to any obstruction

Figure 4: SRD-X7 Reactor Probe sensing element.
key_facts: The Rheonics SRD-X7 can be easily installed and integrated into an existing setup.
Next considerations should be followed to ensure accurate, repeatable and reproducible density and viscosity readings.
The SRD Reactor Probe is suitable for installation in reactors, particularly bioreactors.
The SRD Reactor Probe supports installations with variable insertion length.
The SRD Reactor Probe can be installed in small pipe sizes, such as 1 inch and 1.25 inch ID pipes.
The SRD Reactor Probe can be installed in pipe sizes bigger than 1.5 inches with reduced cross section reduction inside the pipe compared to -X5 sensors.
diagram_labels: Figure 3: SRD-X7 Reactor Probe comparison with SRD-X5.
Figure 4: SRD-X7 Reactor Probe sensing element.
warnings_notes: Ensure sensing element in red is in contact with fluid.
Avoid dead or stagnation zones in the sensing (red) area.
SRD-RP tip needs a clearance of 12 mm [0.5"] to any obstruction.

4. Configuration examples

Next there are some possible configurations for the SRD-RP and its order codes.

3/4" NPT Thread: This is the SRD reactor probe variant with a 3/4" NPT compression fitting in PTFE or SS316L material. Rheonics offers this connection welded in its standard short sensor SRD-X1 (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000409841-srd-x1-34n-npt-3-4-). The advantage the -RP Type sensor offers is the longer insertion of the probe as shown in next two figures for a 150 and 250 L variant.

Figure 9: SRD-RP150-B34N

Figure 10: SRD-RP250-B34N

Table: SRD-RP L: 250 mm B: 3/4" NPT
Part: SRD-RP Reactor Probe Density and Viscosity meter
Order Code: SRD-RP250-B34N

1" NPT: Compression fitting is also available with 1" NPT connection for Stainless Steel and PTFE-PEEK variants.

Figure 11: SRD-RP250-B1N0

Table: SRD-RP L: 250 mm B: 1" NPT
Part: SRD-RP Reactor Probe Density and Viscosity meter
Order Code: SRD-RP250-B1N0

5. How to order the Rheonics Reactor Probe?

Rheonics SRD-X7 Reactor Probe follows next code form.

How to Order Rheonics long insertion viscometer and density meter
key_facts: The SRD-RP reactor probe variants are available with 3/4" NPT compression fittings made of PTFE or SS316L material.
The SRD-RP type sensor offers longer insertion of the probe compared to the standard short sensor SRD-X1.
1" NPT compression fittings are available for the SRD-RP with Stainless Steel and PTFE-PEEK variants.
The Rheonics SRD-X7 Reactor Probe follows a specific ordering code form.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides a guide to the Rheonics SR-Type sensor product code form, detailing configuration options for sensor type, probe variant, probe length, and process connection.
product_names: SRD Inline Density and Viscosity meter
chunks.extracted_text:
Code Form: SR_ - X7 - RP_ - B_
Example: SRD - X7 - RP150 - B34NS

1 Rheonics SR-Type sensor
SRD: Inline Density and Viscosity meter
SRV: Inline Viscometer

2 Sensor Probe Variant
X7: Reactor Probe - RP. Probe variant with ø19 mm body.

3 Reactor Probe RP- Length
Length from sensor probe back end to tip. Rheonics offers 4 variants:
• RP150: 150 mm length
• RP250: 250 mm length
• RP350: 350 mm length
• RP450: 450 mm length

4 Process Connection "B"
Compression fittings defined by client. Common variants are: 3/4" NPT, 1" NPT in Stainless Steel (-S) and PTFE-PEEK (-P). Ask Rheonics Sales Team for availability of other connections.
• B34NP: 3/4" NPT PTFE fitting
• B1N0P: 1" NPT PTFE fitting
• B34NS: 3/4" NPT Stainless Steel fitting
• B1N0S: 1" NPT Stainless Steel fitting

The appropriate RFQ page for SRD density and viscosity meter (https://rheonics.com/request-for-quotation/srd/) should be used to configure other options of the sensor, like density and viscosity range, calibration accuracy, pressure rating, etc.

References
(https://de.vwr.com/store/catalog/product.jsp?catalog_number=COWIPC701.100.19)
[1]: Rheonics long insertion viscometer (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000411102-srv-x5-long-insertion-probes)
key_facts: The Rheonics SR-Type sensor product code includes fields for sensor type, probe variant, probe length, and process connection.
SRD sensors measure inline density and viscosity, while SRV sensors measure inline viscosity.
The X7 Reactor Probe (RP) variant features a 19 mm body diameter.
Reactor Probe (RP) lengths are available in 150 mm, 250 mm, 350 mm, and 450 mm.
Process connections are compression fittings, with common variants being 3/4" NPT and 1" NPT made of Stainless Steel or PTFE-PEEK.
Additional sensor options for SRD, such as density and viscosity range, calibration accuracy, and pressure rating, are configured via the Rheonics RFQ page.
warnings_notes: Ask Rheonics Sales Team for availability of other connections for Process Connection "B".
The appropriate RFQ page for SRD density and viscosity meter should be used to configure other options of the sensor, like density and viscosity range, calibration accuracy, pressure rating, etc.
